name: generic_enterprise_app_architecture
purpose: Root architectural policy manifest. Defines the layer stack, module types, tech stack, codegen conventions, and references to rule files. All generation phases read this file first.
dependency_model: view -> controller -> service -> domain -> data_access; service -> adapter; controller -> query_service -> data_access
global_rules: No module may violate declared layer dependencies.
Global design and persistence conventions are resolved from referenced rule files.
Module-level overrides are allowed only when explicitly declared and justified.
Each public method on a Service class corresponds to exactly one use case — do not aggregate unrelated operations into one service.
Reads (QueryService) bypass the domain layer and go directly to data_access. Writes (Service) must pass through the domain layer.

events.description: DomainEvent<T> base type and DomainEventPublisher interface live in shared — not per module

artifacts.name: {Module}RequestDto
artifacts.description: Input shape for create and update operations

artifacts.name: {Module}ResponseDto
artifacts.description: Output shape returned by write operations

artifacts.name: {Module}ReadDto
artifacts.description: Read model returned by QueryService; shaped for the consuming screen or endpoint

artifacts.name: {Verb}{Module}Command
artifacts.description: Typed input for a single write operation; one command per use case

artifacts.name: {Module}Events
artifacts.description: Specific event payload types for this module; each is a typed alias of DomainEvent<T>

rules: No business logic
No direct entity exposure outside this layer
RequestDto and ResponseDto suffix for write operations
ReadDto suffix for query results
Command suffix for write operation inputs — one Command per Service method
Event types are typed aliases of DomainEvent<T> — no extra classes; suffix with Event (e.g. [event]Event)
Base DomainEvent<T> lives in shared/events/domain-event.ts — not per module

artifacts.name: I{Module}Repository
artifacts.description: Repository contract consumed by the service layer; defines write and load operations

artifacts.name: {Module}Repository
artifacts.description: Raw SQL implementation of the repository interface

artifacts.name: {Module}Mapper
artifacts.description: Translates between DB rows and domain entities; owns column-name mapping

rules: Raw SQL only — no ORM
Parameterized queries always — no string concatenation
Repository maps rows to domain entities via Mapper; never exposes raw DB rows
Mapper handles all snake_case to camelCase translation and type coercions
Mapper must not contain business logic — field translation only
No business logic in Repository
Default columns on every insert: id (UUID), created_at, updated_at
Add version_no integer (default 0) when module requires optimistic locking

artifacts.name: {Module}Entity
artifacts.description: Domain entity owning object state, invariants, and state transitions

artifacts.name: {Module}Validator
artifacts.description: Pure business validation rules; throws domain errors on violation

artifacts.name: {Module}StateMachine
artifacts.description: Lifecycle state transition guards (workflow modules only)

artifacts.name: {Module}Rules
artifacts.description: Decision and policy evaluation logic (rule_engine modules only)

rules: Pure business logic only — no DB calls, no HTTP calls
No dependency on data_access, service, controller, or adapter layers
Entity owns object identity, state, invariants, and legal state transitions
Entity exposes a rehydrate() factory for repository reconstruction and a create() factory for new instances
StateMachine artifact required for workflow module type
Rules artifact required for rule_engine module type

service.description: Write-side orchestration. Each class method corresponds to exactly one use case (e.g. [module]Service.execute()). For reads, use QueryService instead.

artifacts.name: I{Module}Service
artifacts.description: Public write contract exposed to controllers and other modules

artifacts.name: {Module}Service
artifacts.description: Write use-case orchestration: authorize → load → domain behavior → save → emit event

rules: One public method per use case — do not add unrelated methods to the same service class
Receives I{Module}Repository via constructor injection
Receives domain classes via constructor injection
Cross-module calls via the target module's service interface only — never its repository or domain directly
Owns transaction boundary — controllers must not manage transactions
Emits business events for workflow_state_changes, approval_decisions, policy_exceptions (see business-events.md)
Authorization checks performed here — never in controller or domain
Must not perform reads for response shaping — delegate to QueryService after write completes

query_service.description: Read-side only. Bypasses the domain layer and queries data_access directly. Returns read DTOs shaped for the consuming screen or endpoint.

artifacts.name: {Module}QueryService
artifacts.description: Stateless read orchestration; queries data_access directly and returns ReadDto

rules: Stateless — no per-request mutable state
Reads only — must not write to the database
Bypasses domain layer — no entity rehydration unless domain behavior is genuinely needed for the read
Returns ReadDto shaped for the consuming screen — not the domain entity
Cross-module reads via the target module's QueryService only
Constructor may receive: db client, logger, config, cache — nothing else

artifacts.name: {Module}Controller
artifacts.description: HTTP request handler; parses input, calls service or query_service, shapes response

rules: Receives I{Module}Service via constructor injection for writes
Receives {Module}QueryService via constructor injection for reads
Uses Command for write inputs and ReadDto for read responses
Input validation at this boundary only — DTO-level, not business validation
No business logic
No transaction management
Authentication handled here or at gateway — not in service or domain

wiring.order: Controller <- Service <- Repository + Domain; Controller <- QueryService <- Repository

tests.service: unit test with repository mock and domain mock
tests.domain: unit test — pure functions, no mocks needed
